provisions.title: AMENDING THE DOMINICAN NATIONAL CONSTABULARY

      ARTICLE 1. There is hereby appropriated from fund’s in

      MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DO;\I[INGO.
                     Executive Order No. 809.
          AMENDING T H E DOMINICAN NATIONAL

                     CONSTABULARY LAW.

    By virtue of t h e powers vested in the Military Governmelnt
of Santo Domingo, the following Executive Onder is herelby pro-
mulgated :
   ARTICLE 1. Executive Order No. 800, entitle,d ‘‘Dominican

                                -470-

Xational Constabulary Law”, is hcrehy ~,menctedin t h e following
mannler :

     A.   To Article 2 is added t h e foillowing.

     “The Constabullary shall operate only under the c o m n a n d of
its own officers and non-commissioned officers.’’

    2. Article 3 to read a s folllows:
    “The Dominican Kialtional Constabulary shall consist of ~ u c h
forces a s may from time l o time be fixeld by law.”

     C.   Article 5 to rea’d as foll!ows:

     “The Colonel commanding t h e Dominican National Constab-
ulary shall have authority to appoint, promote or reduce all its
non-commipsioned officei“s, subject to regulations issued in accor-
dance with Article 24 of t h e present law.”
     D. Article 10 t o read as follows:

       “When t h e services of t h e Dominican National Constabulary
are requisitioned by duly constituted authority, other t h a n judi-
cial, t h e requisition shall be directed to t h e seaior Corist~abulary
officer or non-commissioned officer present, citing therein t h e
law authorizing t h e services, and t h e purpose, authority, decision
or act by virtue of which it was made. Said requisition shall be
i n writing, dated a n a signled. It shall be obiligiactory on t h e said
senior officer o r non-commissioned officer, if it is in conformity
w i t h t h e above conditions.”

     E.   Articlle 13 to read a s follows:

      “The Dominican National Constabulary officers, of every
rank, shall have the authority, anld sbadl act in t h e capacity, of
juldiicid police and assistants t o t h e Ministerio Publico, anld when
so acting shall be governed by t h e Regulations issuad in accor-
dance with Article 24 of the present law.”

     F. Artiole 22 to relaid as follows:
     “The Dominican National Constabulary shall receive such
rates of pay and allowances as m a y be fixed from time t o time by
jaw.”

     ARTICLE 2 . All laws or parts of liaws in conflict herewith       ,

are hereby rcyealed.

                                     S. S . ROBISON,
                             Rear Admiral, United States Navy,
                            JI:!itul- Gov-,~lior of Santo Domingo.

      MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMIN GO.
                      Executive Order No. 810.
         ANENDING T H E LAW GOVERNING GUXRDAS
                     CAMPESTRES.

     By virtue of t h e powers vested in t h e Military Governjment
iaf Santo Domingo, t h e foillowing Executive Order is hereby pro-
 mulgated :
     ARTICLE 1. The Law governing Guardas Campestres,
Executive Order No. 800, as amended, is hereby f u r t h e r amended
as follows :
    At t h e end of Article 26 shall be aidded the following:
    “Said Executive Order shall remain in full force and effect,
subject t o action by t h e Legislative Power.”

                                         S . S . ROBISON,
